title: [lichen_l2.c:1537-1550] SECURITY: CRC32 fallback when has_link_key=false provides no authentication
description:
Security finding: When link_ctx.has_link_key is false, rx_link_key_ptr is NULL, and lichen_link_rx() falls back to CRC32 verification (lines 1537-1543).

Attack scenario:
1. Current code always has has_link_key=false (no link key provisioning)
2. Frames with 4-byte MIC are verified with CRC32 only
3. CRC32 provides integrity (detects corruption) but NOT authentication
4. Attacker can craft valid CRC32 frames that pass verification

Impact: Currently mitigated because peer_table is empty so all frames fail at signature check first. However:
- If a peer is added with a pubkey
- And no link_key is provisioned
- Frames would authenticate via signature but use CRC32 for MIC
- An attacker observing a valid signed frame could replay it (if replay window allows)

The comment correctly notes this (lines 1539-1544) but does not warn that this is the CURRENT PRODUCTION STATE.

Mitigation:
1. Provision link_key alongside peer_pubkey in lichen_peer_add()
2. Add LOG_WRN when processing frame without link_key
3. Consider requiring link_key for production builds (BUILD_ASSERT)
close_reason: Documented: CRC32 is gated by CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_INSECURE_CRC32_MIC with compile-time warning. Updated SECURITY comment documenting that signature verification provides authentication when CRC32 is used for integrity only.
